title: Is it a private IP?
prompt: Write is_private_ip(ip) returning True if the IP is in a private range: 10.x.x.x, 172.16.x.x through 172.31.x.x, or 192.168.x.x. Otherwise False.

help.plain: Check whether an IP address belongs to one of the three private (internal-network) ranges.
help.analogy: Like telling apart an office extension from a public phone number — private IPs never appear on the open internet.
help.nudge: Split the IP on '.', turn the first two parts into numbers, and check the ranges. Watch the 172 range: only .16 through .31.

details.restate: Decide if an IPv4 address is private (used inside home/office networks) rather than public. Private = 10.x.x.x, 172.16-31.x.x, or 192.168.x.x.

terms.meaning: an address reserved for internal networks, never routed on the public internet
